meta.title: Stock News Sources & Bias Reference
meta.purpose: Source-to-bias mapping for a daily stock-news scanner. Tag each scanned story by its outlet so the UI can show e.g. 'CNN - Lean Left' and downstream analysis can weight or filter by bias and reliability.

bias_scale.Lean Left: Moderate left tilt in story selection and framing.
bias_scale.Center: Balanced or minimal political lean; mixed or neutral framing.
bias_scale.Lean Right: Moderate right tilt in story selection and framing.
bias_scale.Corporate / Promotional: Primary source written by/for a company; inherently company-positive, not a political lean.
bias_scale.Primary / Official: Government or regulatory primary source; effectively non-partisan factual data.
bias_scale.User-generated / Sentiment: Crowd/social content; reflects sentiment, not vetted editorial bias.

reliability_scale.Mostly factual: Generally reliable; occasional spin or sensational headlines.
reliability_scale.Mixed / Varies: Quality varies by author or piece (crowd-sourced or promotional).
reliability_scale.Primary: Raw data/filings - reliable but must be interpreted, and PR is self-interested.

rating_sources: AllSides (allsides.com) - 5-point Left/Lean Left/Center/Lean Right/Right scale; blind bias surveys + editorial review.
Ad Fontes Media (adfontesmedia.com) - Media Bias Chart; rates bias and reliability on a grid.
Media Bias/Fact Check (mediabiasfactcheck.com) - bias category + factual-reporting rating.
how_to_use: Match a story's source domain against each outlet's 'domains' list to assign the 'bias' tag.
Store BOTH 'bias' (political tag for display) and 'reliability' + 'type' (for weighting/filtering).
Prefer tagging by the ORIGINAL source, not the aggregator that surfaced it (e.g. a Reuters story on Yahoo Finance should tag as Reuters).
When a domain is an aggregator or wire reprint, fall back to the syndicating outlet if identifiable.
For opinion/editorial pieces, use the outlet's opinion-section rating where 'bias_opinion' is provided.
caveats: Bias labels reflect the CONSENSUS of AllSides, Ad Fontes, and Media Bias/Fact Check from a US-audience perspective; these orgs sometimes disagree and ratings are revised over time. Treat labels as directional, not absolute.
Political bias is NOT the same as factual accuracy. A source can be biased but accurate, or centrist but unreliable. Always use the 'reliability' field alongside 'bias'.
Many outlets differ sharply between NEWS and OPINION/EDITORIAL (e.g. Wall Street Journal, New York Times). Tag by section when you can; 'bias_opinion' notes the split.
Wire services (Reuters, AP) and primary/official sources (SEC, Fed, BLS) are the most neutral and the highest-value for factual market data.
Corporate press releases (PR Newswire, Business Wire, GlobeNewswire, company IR) are primary but inherently PROMOTIONAL - verify against independent reporting before treating claims as fact.
Social / user-generated feeds (Reddit, StockTwits, X) reflect sentiment and are unvetted, with real pump-and-dump / manipulation risk. Use as a sentiment signal, never as fact.

For stock relevance, 'bias' also has non-political dimensions: sensationalism/clickbait (e.g. Benzinga, Motley Fool headlines) and promotion (small-cap PR, some Seeking Alpha contributor pieces). The 'notes' field flags these.
Geopolitical/macro stories from general outlets matter because they move sectors (e.g. Middle East conflict -> oil -> energy equities like Exxon). These outlets are included for that reason.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.notes: Global newswire; among the most neutral, fact-driven sources. Breaking market, M&A, macro and geopolitical news. Top-tier for stocks - often the first and most reliable on company and economic news.

outlets.name: Associated Press
outlets.notes: Global newswire; AllSides rates Lean Left, others Center - widely reliable. Markets, economy, and geopolitics. High-value primary reporting that moves stocks.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.notes: Premier financial news and data. MBFC rates Left-Center; markets coverage is widely seen as rigorous and broadly centrist. Deep company, markets, rates, and deal coverage - core source for stocks.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.notes: Real-time markets TV and web. AllSides Lean Left. Heavy on earnings, Fed, single-stock moves and management interviews; fast but can be noisy/sensational. Core stock source.

outlets.name: Wall Street Journal
outlets.notes: Premier business daily. NEWS desk rated Center; OPINION/editorial page Lean Right - tag by section. Deep coverage of companies, deals, and the economy. Core stock source.
outlets.section_note: News desk and Opinion/Editorial differ - tag by section where possible.

outlets.name: Financial Times
outlets.notes: Global business daily. MBFC Least Biased / high factual. Strong on global markets, macro, and corporates. Core stock source, especially international.

outlets.name: MarketWatch
outlets.notes: Retail-focused markets news and commentary. Generally Center (some Left-Center). Fast market recaps, single-stock movers, and personal-finance angles.

outlets.name: Barron's
outlets.notes: Investing weekly. Center / least-biased. Stock picking, analysis, and markets features - investment-oriented.

outlets.name: Forbes
outlets.notes: Business and markets. Center overall, but note a large CONTRIBUTOR network whose quality and slant vary by author - tag staff vs contributor where possible.

outlets.name: Business Insider
outlets.notes: Business/tech/markets, faster and more casual. AllSides Lean Left. Markets data pages plus trend and company stories; headlines can be sensational.

outlets.name: Yahoo Finance
outlets.notes: Largest retail finance portal and AGGREGATOR; mixes own content with syndicated wires. Bias depends on the original source - retag to the syndicating outlet when identifiable. Great for quotes, filings, transcripts.

outlets.name: Investor's Business Daily
outlets.notes: Markets and growth-stock focus (CANSLIM). MBFC rates Right; high factual. Technical/growth stock coverage and screens.

outlets.name: Seeking Alpha
outlets.notes: Crowd-sourced investor analysis. MBFC Right-Center, high factual on news, but CONTRIBUTOR articles vary wildly and can be talking-their-book / promotional. Useful for theses, but verify.

outlets.name: The Motley Fool
outlets.notes: Retail investing content. MBFC Left-Center, high factual, BUT strongly subscription/promotion-driven with clickbait headlines. Treat recommendations as marketing.

outlets.name: Benzinga
outlets.notes: Fast markets news, movers, and analyst-rating roundups. MBFC Center. Speed-oriented; carries PR/sponsored and small-cap promotional content - flag sponsored items.

outlets.name: Morningstar
outlets.notes: Independent investment research and fund/stock ratings. Data-driven, low political lean. Valuation, moat, and fair-value analysis - high-value for fundamentals.

outlets.name: Investopedia
outlets.notes: Educational reference plus markets news. Center / least biased. Good for definitions and explainers behind a story.

outlets.name: TheStreet
outlets.notes: Markets news and commentary (founded by Jim Cramer). Center / Left-Center. Single-stock and markets coverage.

outlets.name: Kiplinger
outlets.notes: Personal finance and investing. MBFC Least Biased / high factual. Investing guidance and markets explainers.

outlets.name: Zacks
outlets.notes: Quant research, earnings-estimate revisions, and rankings. Data-driven, low political lean. Strong for estimate-revision and earnings signals.

outlets.name: Fox Business
outlets.notes: Business news TV/web. AllSides Lean Right. Markets, economy, and policy with a business-friendly, right-leaning frame.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.notes: Major general/breaking news. AllSides Lean Left. Matters for stocks via macro, policy, and geopolitics (e.g. Middle East conflict -> oil -> energy names like Exxon).

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.notes: Major general news, conservative frame. AllSides Right. Markets relevance via policy, regulation, energy, and geopolitics; separate news vs opinion.

outlets.name: New York Times
outlets.notes: Newspaper of record. NEWS Lean Left, OPINION Left - tag by section. Strong DealBook/business desk; macro, policy, tech, and corporate investigations move stocks.
outlets.section_note: News desk and Opinion/Editorial differ - tag by section where possible.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.notes: Major national daily. AllSides Lean Left. Policy, regulation, and tech coverage with market impact.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.notes: Public radio news. AllSides Lean Left, high factual. Economy and policy coverage; measured tone.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.notes: National broadcast news. AllSides Lean Left. Macro, policy, and breaking events with market spillover.

outlets.name: ABC News
outlets.notes: National broadcast news. AllSides Lean Left. General and economic news relevant to markets.

outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.notes: National broadcast news. AllSides Lean Left. General and economic news relevant to markets.

outlets.name: Politico
outlets.notes: Washington policy and politics. AllSides Lean Left. High value for REGULATION/legislation that moves sectors (health, energy, tech, defense, finance).

outlets.name: Axios
outlets.notes: Brief, scoop-driven news. AllSides Center (some Lean Left). Fast business, tech, and policy scoops with market relevance.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.notes: Congressional and policy news. AllSides Center. Useful for legislation/regulation affecting sectors.

outlets.name: The Economist
outlets.notes: Global affairs and economics weekly. AllSides Lean Left socially, but classically liberal / pro-free-market on economics. Strong macro and global-markets framing.

outlets.name: BBC News
outlets.notes: Global news. AllSides Center (US view). Strong international and macro coverage relevant to global equities and commodities.

outlets.name: The Guardian
outlets.notes: UK-based global news. AllSides Lean Left. International, energy/climate, and policy coverage with market relevance.

outlets.name: STAT News
outlets.notes: Health, pharma, and biotech journalism. Low political lean, high quality. Critical for biotech/pharma catalysts (FDA, trials) that move those stocks.

outlets.name: Endpoints News
outlets.notes: Biopharma industry trade news. Center / industry-focused. Drug approvals, trials, and deals that move biotech equities.

outlets.name: The Verge
outlets.notes: Consumer tech and policy. AllSides Lean Left. Product, regulation, and platform news affecting big-tech stocks.

outlets.name: TechCrunch
outlets.notes: Startups, venture, and tech-company news. Roughly Center/Lean Left. Funding, product, and M&A news relevant to tech equities.

outlets.name: OilPrice.com
outlets.notes: Energy and commodities news/analysis. Industry-focused, low political lean; analysis quality varies. Oil/gas pricing and supply news for energy equities.

outlets.name: SEC EDGAR
outlets.notes: Official filings database (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, 13D/G, Form 4). Non-partisan primary source - the ground truth behind many stock stories.

outlets.name: Federal Reserve
outlets.notes: FOMC statements, minutes, and speeches. Non-partisan. Rate decisions and guidance move the whole market.

outlets.name: Bureau of Labor Statistics
outlets.notes: Jobs report (NFP) and CPI inflation data. Non-partisan primary data; major market-moving releases.

outlets.name: Bureau of Economic Analysis
outlets.notes: GDP, PCE inflation, trade data. Non-partisan primary data with market impact.

outlets.name: U.S. Treasury
outlets.notes: Treasury yields, auctions, and policy. Non-partisan primary source for rates-sensitive sectors.

outlets.name: PR Newswire
outlets.notes: Company press releases (earnings, products, deals). Primary but company-positive/promotional - the company's own framing. Verify claims independently.

outlets.name: Business Wire
outlets.notes: Company press-release distribution. Primary but promotional. Often the original text behind a 'company announced' story.

outlets.name: GlobeNewswire
outlets.notes: Company press-release distribution, common for small/mid-caps. Primary but promotional; watch for paid small-cap promotion.

outlets.name: Reddit (r/wallstreetbets, r/stocks, r/investing)
outlets.notes: Retail-investor forums. Not editorial bias - a SENTIMENT signal. High meme/squeeze and pump risk; useful for crowd positioning, never as fact.

outlets.name: StockTwits
outlets.notes: Ticker-tagged social stream. Sentiment signal (bullish/bearish tags). Promotional and manipulation risk; treat as sentiment only.

outlets.name: X / Twitter (FinTwit)
outlets.notes: Real-time posts from journalists, analysts, and retail ('FinTwit'). Fast but unvetted; verify against primary sources. Sentiment and breaking-rumor signal.
